title: Background runtime strategy: beginBackgroundTask for T0, BGProcessing for T1+
description:
Make the iOS background execution model explicit. T0 runs under a short UIApplication.beginBackgroundTask to finish during app-to-background transition. T1+ are scheduled via BGProcessingTaskRequest with requiresExternalPower=true.

WHY: iOS suspends backgrounded apps by default. If a download completes and the user immediately backgrounds the app, T0 work must complete or be checkpointed. beginBackgroundTask gives ~30 seconds of runtime. BGProcessingTask gives longer runtime but is opportunistic.

WHAT TO DO:
1. In AnalysisWorkScheduler, when processing a T0 job:
   - Call UIApplication.shared.beginBackgroundTask before starting the runner.
   - On completion or expiration, call endBackgroundTask.
   - If the background task expires before T0 finishes, the runner is cancelled and checkpoints partial progress.

2. In BackgroundProcessingService (Playhead/Services/AnalysisCoordinator/BackgroundProcessingService.swift):
   - Register a new BGProcessingTaskRequest for pre-analysis recovery (com.playhead.app.preanalysis.recovery).
   - requiresExternalPower = true for T1+ jobs.
   - Handler delegates to AnalysisWorkScheduler.resumeOnLaunch().
   - Scheduled after T0 completes if more tiers are available.

3. Reconciliation runs at:
   - App launch (already handled by resumeOnLaunch)
   - Background URL session completion handler (in EpisodeDownloadDelegate or AppDelegate)
   - After model install completion (in AssetProvider callback)
   - First launch after app upgrade (check stored app version)

TESTING:
- Simulate background task expiration: verify T0 checkpoints partial progress.
- Verify BGProcessingTask scheduled after T0 for T1+ work.
- Verify reconciliation picks up stalled jobs on launch.

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisWorkScheduler.swift
- Modify: Playhead/Services/AnalysisCoordinator/BackgroundProcessingService.swift
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/PreAnalysis/BackgroundRuntimeTests.swift

title: Add charging state to CapabilitySnapshot and CapabilitiesService
description:
FOUNDATION: CapabilitiesService currently tracks thermal state and Low Power Mode but NOT charging state. The entire pre-analysis tier system depends on observing charging transitions -- T1+ work only runs while charging, and the scheduler needs to pause/resume when the charger is connected/disconnected.

WHY THIS IS FIRST: Every other bead in this feature depends on knowing whether the device is charging. Without this, the scheduler cannot make eligibility decisions for deferred-lane work.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Add isCharging: Bool to CapabilitySnapshot (Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshot.swift:8-47). Add after isLowPowerMode to keep the struct logically grouped.
2. Update CapabilitiesService.captureSnapshot() (CapabilitiesService.swift:108-129) to read UIDevice.current.batteryState. Need import UIKit and device.isBatteryMonitoringEnabled = true. Charging = .charging or .full.
3. Add UIDevice.batteryStateDidChangeNotification observer in startObserving() (line ~78-102) alongside existing thermal and power mode observers. Observer calls refreshSnapshot() which broadcasts to all continuations.
4. Fix all existing call sites that construct CapabilitySnapshot.

TESTING:
- Unit test: construct snapshot with isCharging true/false, assert the field.
- Verify CapabilitiesService broadcasts on batteryState change.
- Confirm BackgroundProcessingService (which has its own UIDevice charging check at lines 392-397) still works.

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshot.swift
- Modify: Playhead/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitiesService.swift
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/Capabilities/CapabilitySnapshotTests.swift

title: Observability: OSSignposter intervals and diagnostic metrics
description:
Add instrumentation for tuning and diagnostics. Without observability, this feature is impossible to tune by intuition.

SIGNPOSTER INTERVALS (OSSignposter):
- queue_wait: time from job enqueue to job start.
- job_duration: time from job start to outcome.
- download_to_cue: time from download completion to first SkipCue materialized.
- handoff_latency: time from playbackStarted() to lease transfer complete.
- thermal_pause: duration of thermal pauses.
- stage_duration: per-stage timing (spool, features, transcript, detect, materialize).

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use os.signpost with OSSignposter (iOS 16+). Create a shared signposter instance:
  private static let signposter = OSSignposter(subsystem: "com.playhead", category: "PreAnalysis")

Begin/end intervals at key points:
- AnalysisWorkScheduler: queue_wait (enqueue -> job start), handoff_latency
- AnalysisJobRunner: job_duration, stage_duration per stage
- SkipCueMaterializer: download_to_cue (log timestamp at enqueue, compute delta at first cue)

DIAGNOSTIC METRICS (logged, not shipped -- for development/debugging):
- Play-start cue readiness rate: episodes where SkipCues existed at beginEpisode.
- Mean time to first cue.
- Tier completion rate.
- Thermal pause frequency.
- Duplicate cue suppression count.
Log these via Logger at .info level with structured key-value pairs.

FEATURE FLAG: Add a preAnalysisEnabled Bool to a settings/config that can disable the entire feature. Default true. Useful for debugging and A/B testing later.

TESTING:
- Verify signpost intervals are emitted (check OSSignposter API does not crash).
- Verify diagnostic log messages appear with expected keys.
- Verify feature flag disables pre-analysis when false.

FILES:
- Create: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/PreAnalysisInstrumentation.swift
- Modify: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisWorkScheduler.swift (add signposts)
- Modify: Playhead/Services/AnalysisJobRunner/AnalysisJobRunner.swift (add signposts)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/PreAnalysis/InstrumentationTests.swift

title: Add analysis_jobs table to AnalysisStore (versioned job model)
description:
Replace the v1 PreAnalysisRecord with a versioned job model. The durable key is a workKey derived from fingerprint + analysisVersion + modelSet + jobType, not just episodeId. This prevents duplicate-enqueue data loss (INSERT OR REPLACE wiping progress), handles re-downloads with changed fingerprints, and supports analysis version upgrades.

WHY VERSIONED JOBS: A single row keyed by episodeId cannot represent re-downloads, fingerprint changes, model availability changes, retries, or lease ownership. The workKey provides idempotency -- duplicate enqueues with the same workKey are no-ops; re-downloads with a new fingerprint create a new job and supersede the old.

SCHEMA (analysis_jobs table):
  jobId TEXT PRIMARY KEY (UUID)
  jobType TEXT NOT NULL (preRollWarmup)
  episodeId TEXT NOT NULL
  podcastId TEXT
  analysisAssetId TEXT (links to analysis_assets after runner creates it)
  workKey TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE (fingerprint|analysisVersion|modelSet|jobType)
  sourceFingerprint TEXT NOT NULL
  downloadId TEXT NOT NULL
  priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
  desiredCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL
  featureCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
  transcriptCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
  cueCoverageSec REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
  state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT queued (queued|running|blocked|paused|complete|superseded|failed)
  attemptCount INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
  nextEligibleAt REAL (unix timestamp; null = eligible now)
  leaseOwner TEXT (preAnalysis|playback|nil)
  leaseExpiresAt REAL (unix timestamp)
  lastErrorCode TEXT
  createdAt REAL NOT NULL
  updatedAt REAL NOT NULL

INDEX on (state, priority DESC, createdAt ASC) for scheduler queries.
INDEX on workKey for idempotency checks.
INDEX on episodeId for playback handoff lookups.

CRUD METHODS to add to AnalysisStore:
- insertJob (idempotent on workKey -- no-op if exists with same workKey)
- fetchNextEligibleJob(now:, capabilities:) -- the eligibility-aware query
- updateJobProgress(jobId:, featureCoverage:, transcriptCoverage:, cueCoverage:, state:)
- updateJobLease(jobId:, leaseOwner:, leaseExpiresAt:)
- releaseJobLease(jobId:)
- supersedeJobsForEpisode(episodeId:, exceptJobId:)
- deleteExpiredJobs(olderThan:)
- fetchJobByWorkKey(workKey:)
- fetchJobsForEpisode(episodeId:)

TESTING:
- Insert + fetch round-trip
- Duplicate workKey insert is no-op (not replace)
- fetchNextEligibleJob respects: state filter, nextEligibleAt, priority ordering, charging requirement
- Supersede old jobs when new fingerprint arrives
- Lease acquire/release/expire

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Persistence/AnalysisStore/AnalysisStore.swift (add AnalysisJob struct, DDL, CRUD)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Persistence/AnalysisJobStoreTests.swift

title: Integration test suite for pre-analysis feature
description:
Comprehensive integration tests covering the scenarios identified in review. These go beyond unit tests and verify cross-component behavior.

TEST SCENARIOS:
1. Duplicate enqueue: same episode, same fingerprint -> no duplicate job.
2. Re-download with new fingerprint: new content version -> old job superseded, new job created.
3. Paused T1 does NOT block eligible T0: enqueue T1-paused and T0-pending, verify T0 is selected.
4. Model becomes available mid-queue: job in blocked:modelUnavailable -> becomes eligible when model installed.
5. Download completes while app is backgrounded: verify reconciler picks up un-enqueued download on launch.
6. User taps Play during background job commit: lease transfer, playback resumes from frontier.
7. Overlap between preloaded cues and live-generated cues: same ad region detected twice -> one cue (dedup).
8. Charger removed mid-T2: pause + resume on reconnect.
9. Stale job after app upgrade: version reconciliation supersedes old job.
10. Audio file evicted by LRU while job is paused: blocked:missingFile -> unblocked if re-cached.

APPROACH: Use test fixtures with stubbed services (same pattern as existing PlayheadTests). Each test sets up initial state in a temp AnalysisStore, runs the scheduler/runner/reconciler, and asserts final state.

FILES:
- Create: PlayheadTests/IntegrationTests/PreAnalysisIntegrationTests.swift

title: Create SkipCue materialization layer
description:
Raw AdWindow rows are internal detection artifacts. Separately materialize SkipCue rows as the user-safe cue layer. This solves two problems: (1) dedup when live detection re-emits overlapping windows, and (2) confidence-threshold gating so low-confidence candidates never fire as skip cues.

WHY NOT RAW ADWINDOWS: Loading raw AdWindows in SkipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() mixes detection output with product UX. When live playback analysis emits overlapping windows for the same ad region that pre-analysis already found, you get duplicates. The SkipCue layer provides stable hashing, overlap-merge rules, and confidence thresholds.

SCHEMA (skip_cues table):
  cueId TEXT PRIMARY KEY (UUID)
  analysisAssetId TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES analysis_assets(id)
  cueHash TEXT NOT NULL (stable hash for dedup: SHA256 of assetId|startTime|endTime rounded to 1s)
  startTime REAL NOT NULL
  endTime REAL NOT NULL
  confidence REAL NOT NULL
  source TEXT NOT NULL (preAnalysis|liveDetection)
  advertiser TEXT
  isEligible INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (meets confidence + span thresholds)
  createdAt REAL NOT NULL
  UNIQUE(analysisAssetId, cueHash)

MATERIALIZATION LOGIC:
- Called by AnalysisJobRunner after hot-path detection (when outputPolicy == .writeWindowsAndCues).
- For each AdWindow above confidence threshold (0.65 = enterThreshold from SkipPolicyConfig):
  1. Compute cueHash from rounded start/end times.
  2. Check if cue with same hash exists -- if so, update confidence if higher, skip otherwise.
  3. Overlap-merge: if new cue overlaps an existing cue by >50%, merge into the wider range and take max confidence.
  4. Apply eligibility: confidence >= 0.65 AND span >= 15s (or >= 0.85 confidence for short spans).
- INSERT OR IGNORE on cueHash uniqueness constraint.

CRUD METHODS to add to AnalysisStore:
- insertOrUpdateSkipCue(cue:) -- idempotent on cueHash
- fetchEligibleSkipCues(assetId:) -- WHERE isEligible = 1
- fetchAllSkipCues(assetId:)
- deleteSkipCues(assetId:) -- for cleanup

SKIP ORCHESTRATOR CHANGE:
In SkipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() (Playhead/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipOrchestrator.swift:219-244), after loading feature windows, load eligible SkipCues and convert to managed windows. This replaces the raw AdWindow preloading from v1.

Live playback detection refines or supersedes cues via the same cueHash dedup -- no double-skips.

TESTING:
- Materialize cues from AdWindows, verify only eligible ones returned.
- Duplicate cueHash insert is no-op.
- Overlap merge: two overlapping windows -> one wider cue.
- SkipOrchestrator.beginEpisode() loads pre-computed cues and has them ready.
- Live detection re-emitting same region does not create duplicate cue.

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Persistence/AnalysisStore/AnalysisStore.swift (SkipCue struct, DDL, CRUD)
- Create: Playhead/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipCueMaterializer.swift
- Modify: Playhead/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipOrchestrator.swift (beginEpisode preload)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipCueMaterializerTests.swift
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/SkipOrchestrator/SkipOrchestratorPreloadTests.swift

title: Create AnalysisWorkScheduler with eligibility-aware job selection
description:
The central scheduler that selects the highest-priority ELIGIBLE job under current constraints. Replaces the v1 PreAnalysisScheduler FIFO approach which had a correctness bug: a paused T1 at the front of the queue blocked eligible T0 work behind it.

WHY ELIGIBILITY-AWARE: Simple FIFO with fetchActionableRecords().first returns if the first record is charging-only and the device is not charging, blocking all subsequent work. The scheduler must select from eligible work only.

TWO LOGICAL LANES:
- Immediate lane: T0 / explicit downloads / Up Next episodes. Always eligible (no charging requirement).
- Deferred lane: T1+ charging-only extension work. Eligible only when isCharging && !shouldThrottleAnalysis.

JOB SELECTION QUERY (delegated to AnalysisStore.fetchNextEligibleJob):
  SELECT * FROM analysis_jobs
  WHERE state IN ('queued', 'paused')
  AND (nextEligibleAt IS NULL OR nextEligibleAt <= :now)
  AND (leaseOwner IS NULL OR leaseExpiresAt < :now)
  AND (
    -- Immediate lane: T0 jobs (desiredCoverageSec <= 90 AND cueCoverageSec < desiredCoverageSec)
    (desiredCoverageSec <= :t0Threshold AND cueCoverageSec < desiredCoverageSec)
    OR
    -- Deferred lane: only if charging and not throttled
    (:isCharging = 1 AND :isThermalOk = 1)
  )
  ORDER BY priority DESC, createdAt ASC
  LIMIT 1

SCHEDULER ACTOR:
  actor AnalysisWorkScheduler {
    init(store:, jobRunner:, capabilitiesService:)
    func enqueue(episodeId:, podcastId:, downloadId:, sourceFingerprint:, isExplicitDownload:, desiredCoverage:)
    func playbackStarted(episodeId:) -- request lease transfer, mark superseded
    func episodeDeleted(episodeId:) -- cancel + delete
    func startObservingCharging() -- kick deferred lane on charge connect
    func resumeOnLaunch() -- reconcile + process queue
  }

TIER ADVANCEMENT:
After AnalysisJobRunner returns an AnalysisOutcome:
- If stopReason == .reachedTarget and cueCoverageSec >= desiredCoverageSec:
  - If next tier exists: create new job with higher desiredCoverage, state=paused (charging-only).
  - If no more tiers: mark complete.
- If stopReason == .blockedByModel: mark blocked:modelUnavailable, set nextEligibleAt.
- If stopReason == .pausedForThermal: mark paused.
- If stopReason == .failed: increment attemptCount, apply exponential backoff to nextEligibleAt.

PREEMPTION: At tier boundaries (between jobs, not mid-job), check if a higher-priority job has arrived. If so, process it first.

CONCURRENCY: One CPU-heavy job at a time. Scheduler loop: fetch eligible -> acquire lease -> run -> update progress -> loop.

LEASE MANAGEMENT:
- Before running: acquire lease (leaseOwner=preAnalysis, leaseExpiresAt=now+5min).
- Periodically renew lease during long jobs.
- On completion: release lease.
- On playbackStarted: request lease transfer -- wait for current job to checkpoint and release.

TESTING:
- Paused T1 does NOT block eligible T0 behind it.
- Explicit downloads prioritized over auto-downloads.
- Charging state gates deferred lane.
- Thermal throttle pauses all work.
- Tier advancement creates next-tier job.
- Exponential backoff on failure.
- Preemption at tier boundary for new high-priority job.
- Lease acquire/release/transfer on playback start.

FILES:
- Create: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisWorkScheduler.swift
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisWorkSchedulerTests.swift

title: Reconciler: blocked states, backoff, and job repair
description:
Add a reconciler that runs at each reconciliation point to repair stale/broken job state. Production background systems need explicit recovery -- a plain retry on next charge is too blunt.

BLOCKED STATES:
- missingFile: audio file evicted by LRU. Resolve from downloadId/fingerprint if re-cached.
- modelUnavailable: Whisper model not downloaded yet. Unblock when model inventory reports ready.
- versionStale: analysis version or model set changed since job created. Supersede old job, create new one.
- leaseLost: job was running state with expired lease (crash recovery). Requeue.

RECONCILER LOGIC (runs at launch, model install, app upgrade, bg URL session completion):
1. Find jobs in running state with expired leases -> mark leaseLost, requeue.
2. Find jobs in blocked:missingFile -> check if audio file exists via DownloadManager.cachedFileURL, unblock if found.
3. Find jobs in blocked:modelUnavailable -> check WhisperKitService.isReady() or ModelInventory status, unblock if ready.
4. Find jobs where workKey no longer matches current analysisVersion+modelSet -> mark versionStale, create replacement.
5. Garbage-collect complete and superseded jobs older than 7 days.
6. Apply exponential backoff: for failed jobs, set nextEligibleAt = now + min(2^attemptCount * 60, 3600) seconds.

TESTING:
- Job in running with expired lease -> requeued after reconciliation.
- Job in blocked:missingFile -> unblocked when file appears.
- Job in blocked:modelUnavailable -> unblocked when model ready.
- Stale workKey -> old job superseded, new job created.
- GC removes old complete/superseded jobs.
- Exponential backoff: attempt 1 = 1min, attempt 2 = 2min, attempt 3 = 4min, capped at 1hr.

FILES:
- Create: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisJobReconciler.swift
- Modify: Playhead/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisWorkScheduler.swift (call reconciler at entry points)
- Modify: Playhead/Persistence/AnalysisStore/AnalysisStore.swift (blocked state queries)
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/PreAnalysis/AnalysisJobReconcilerTests.swift

title: Wire AnalysisWorkScheduler into PlayheadRuntime composition root
description:
Connect all the pieces in the app composition root. PlayheadRuntime creates and wires AnalysisJobRunner, AnalysisWorkScheduler, and connects them to DownloadManager.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Add properties to PlayheadRuntime: downloadManager, analysisJobRunner, analysisWorkScheduler.
2. Initialize AnalysisJobRunner with store, audioService, featureService, transcriptEngine, adDetectionService.
3. Initialize AnalysisWorkScheduler with store, jobRunner, capabilitiesService.
4. Bootstrap DownloadManager and wire it to the scheduler.
5. In the launch Task (after backgroundProcessingService.start()):
   - await downloadManager.bootstrap()
   - await downloadManager.setAnalysisWorkScheduler(scheduler)
   - await analysisWorkScheduler.startObservingCharging()
   - await analysisWorkScheduler.resumeOnLaunch()
6. In playEpisode() (after skipOrchestrator.beginEpisode()):
   - await analysisWorkScheduler.playbackStarted(episodeId: episodeId)
7. AnalysisCoordinator should use AnalysisJobRunner for its own pipeline execution (playback mode) rather than duplicating the spool->features->transcript->detect logic. This may be a larger refactor -- at minimum, coordinator delegates to runner for the core pipeline stages.

CONSIDERATION: The DownloadManager may already be created elsewhere (check if PlayheadRuntime or another service already owns it). If so, just add the scheduler wiring, don't create a second instance.

TESTING:
- Build succeeds with all new wiring.
- App launches without crash.
- Manual test: download an episode, verify analysis job appears in SQLite.

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/App/PlayheadRuntime.swift

title: Wire DownloadManager to AnalysisWorkScheduler
description:
Connect download completion to pre-analysis job creation. Minimal change to DownloadManager -- add DownloadContext and a scheduler reference.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Add DownloadContext struct (podcastId, isExplicitDownload) to DownloadManager.swift.
2. Add optional scheduler reference to DownloadManager (set via setter to avoid init coupling).
3. Update progressiveDownload() signature to accept optional DownloadContext.
4. After performDownload() completes fingerprint computation (line ~296), call scheduler.enqueue() with the download context, fingerprint, and download ID.
5. For background download delegate path (EpisodeDownloadDelegate): cannot call scheduler directly (Sendable delegate, no actor ref). Instead, scheduler reconciles on launch by checking for cached audio files without corresponding analysis jobs.

DOWNLOAD CONTEXT:
  struct DownloadContext: Sendable {
    let podcastId: String?
    let isExplicitDownload: Bool
  }

TESTING:
- Unit test: DownloadContext construction.
- Integration: after progressive download completes, verify an analysis job was enqueued.
- Background delegate path: verify reconciler picks up un-enqueued downloads.

FILES:
- Modify: Playhead/Services/Downloads/DownloadManager.swift
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/Downloads/DownloadManagerPreAnalysisTests.swift

title: Create AnalysisJobRunner actor (bounded-range analysis engine)
description:
Extract a reusable bounded-range analysis engine from AnalysisCoordinator. The runner executes the pipeline (spool -> features -> transcript -> detection) over a Range<TimeInterval> and writes durable stage frontiers. It does not know about playback state, tier policy, or scheduling.

WHY SEPARATE FROM COORDINATOR: The v1 design added preAnalyze() to AnalysisCoordinator, coupling background work to playback-only state (activeEpisodeId, latestSnapshot, shard prioritization). The synthetic PlaybackSnapshot was the giveaway -- background work modeled as fake playback. The next playback optimization could silently break pre-analysis. A dedicated runner gives a reusable core for preRollWarmup, playback hot-path, and future jobs like model-upgrade backfill.

INTERFACE:
  actor AnalysisJobRunner {
    init(store:, audioService:, featureService:, transcriptEngine:, adDetectionService:)
    func run(_ request: AnalysisRangeRequest) async -> AnalysisOutcome
  }

  struct AnalysisRangeRequest: Sendable {
    enum Mode { case preRollWarmup, playback, backgroundBackfill }
    let jobId: String
    let episodeId: String
    let podcastId: String?
    let analysisAssetId: String?
    let audioURL: URL
    let range: Range<TimeInterval>
    let mode: Mode
    let outputPolicy: OutputPolicy
    let priority: TaskPriority
  }

  enum OutputPolicy { case writeWindowsAndCues, writeWindowsAndPushLive, writeWindowsOnly }

  struct AnalysisOutcome: Sendable {
    enum StopReason { case reachedTarget, cancelledByPlayback, pausedForThermal, blockedByModel, failed(String) }
    let assetId: String
    let requestedCoverageSec: Double
    let featureCoverageSec: Double
    let transcriptCoverageSec: Double
    let cueCoverageSec: Double
    let newCueCount: Int
    let stopReason: StopReason
  }

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. The runner resolves or creates an AnalysisAsset + AnalysisSession (reuse resolveSession logic from AnalysisCoordinator.swift:553-628).
2. Decodes audio via AnalysisAudioService, filters shards to the requested range.
3. Runs feature extraction capped at range.upperBound.
4. Runs transcription with a synthetic snapshot (playheadTime: range.lowerBound, isPlaying: false) -- processes shards linearly.
5. Runs hot-path ad detection on resulting transcript chunks.
6. If outputPolicy == .writeWindowsAndCues, also materializes SkipCue rows (see SkipCue bead).
7. Checkpoints durable stage frontiers (feature, transcript, cue coverage) to AnalysisStore after each stage.
8. Returns AnalysisOutcome with actual coverage achieved and stop reason.

QoS: .utility for preRollWarmup, .userInitiated for playback.
Cancellation: checks Task.isCancelled between stages. On cancellation, checkpoints whatever was achieved.

TESTING:
- Run with stub services, verify outcome has correct coverage and stop reason.
- Verify stage frontiers persisted to SQLite after each stage.
- Verify cancellation checkpoints partial progress.
- Verify mode-specific behavior (output policy).

FILES:
- Create: Playhead/Services/AnalysisJobRunner/AnalysisJobRunner.swift
- Create: Playhead/Services/AnalysisJobRunner/AnalysisRangeRequest.swift
- Create: Playhead/Services/AnalysisJobRunner/AnalysisOutcome.swift
- Create: PlayheadTests/Services/AnalysisJobRunner/AnalysisJobRunnerTests.swift
